agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"external-inventory-api","status":"risk","issue":"No timeout configured on outbound HTTP call — a hung upstream connection will block the sync indefinitely, potentially causing goroutine/thread leaks and missed sync windows"},{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"local-database","status":"risk","issue":"No visibility into database write failures vs API fetch failures — both collapse into a single console.log error path, making root-cause analysis difficult"},{"from":"external-inventory-api","to":"inventory-sync-service","status":"risk","issue":"No contract validation on API response shape — if the external API changes its schema, the service may silently write corrupt stock counts to the database"}],"contract_compliance":"Unknown — no response validation or schema checks on external API payloads. Corrupt or partial data could propagate to local database without detection.","data_flow_integrity":"At risk. If a sync fails silently (no alert, no metric), downstream consumers of stock counts will serve stale data with no indication of staleness. There is no last-successful-sync timestamp exposed for consumers to check freshness."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["Logs use console.log instead of structured logging (no JSON, no log levels) — impossible to filter, aggregate, or alert on in any log management system","No correlation ID or run ID per sync cycle — cannot trace a single sync execution across log lines","No logging of sync duration, record count processed, or partial failure details","Error logging captures err.message only — stack traces, HTTP status codes, and upstream response bodies are lost","No log indicating sync START — only completion or failure, so a hung sync produces zero log output"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No sync success/failure counter — cannot calculate reliability rate or trigger alerts","No sync duration histogram — cannot detect performance degradation","No records-processed gauge — cannot detect partial syncs or data volume anomalies","No stale-data gauge (time since last successful sync) — the single most important operational metric for this service","No error-type breakdown (network, timeout, auth, 5xx) — cannot distinguish transient from permanent failures"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed tracing headers on outbound API calls","No trace context linking sync cycles to downstream database writes","No span instrumentation for API call vs DB write phases"]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am would see either 'sync completed' or 'sync failed: <message>' in unstructured logs with no way to determine: how long the sync took, how many records were affected, what HTTP status the API returned, whether the database write succeeded, or how long data has been stale. They would need to SSH in and manually query the database to assess impact."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"External inventory API is down (5xx or unreachable)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s). After exhausting retries, emit a metric and structured error log with HTTP status. If failures persist across multiple sync cycles, trigger an alert."},{"scenario":"External inventory API hangs indefinitely","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a request timeout of 30 seconds. This is the highest-priority fix — a hung connection can cascade: the sync never completes, the next scheduled sync may overlap, and threads/connections leak."},{"scenario":"External inventory API returns partial or malformed data","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate response schema before writing to database. Consider checksums or record count validation against expected inventory size."},{"scenario":"Database write fails mid-sync (partial update)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Wrap database writes in a transaction so stock counts update atomically. A partial sync is worse than no sync — some products show new counts while others show stale counts."},{"scenario":"Sync runs overlap (previous sync still running when next 5-min tick fires)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a mutex or leader-election mechanism to prevent concurrent syncs. At minimum, skip the new cycle if the previous one is still in progress and log a warning."},{"scenario":"Authentication token expires or is revoked","handling":"partial","recommendation":"The generic error log will capture this but it won't be distinguishable from network errors. Log the HTTP status code separately and alert specifically on 401/403."}],"retry_config":"None. Single attempt per sync cycle. If it fails, stale data persists for at least 5 more minutes with no alert.","timeout_config":"None. Outbound HTTP call can hang indefinitely. This is a critical gap.","circuit_breakers":"None. The service will hammer a failing upstream API every 5 minutes indefinitely with no backoff or notification.","graceful_degradation":"None. On failure, stale data is served silently. No staleness indicator is exposed to downstream consumers."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Re-deploy previous container image. No database migrations in this release, so rollback is clean. Verify stock counts are not corrupted before rolling back — if bad data was written, a rollback alone won't fix it.","monitoring_coverage":"None. Zero metrics, no health endpoint, unstructured logs only. There is no way to alert on sync failures, detect stale data, or monitor service health programmatically.","on_call_documentation":"Insufficient. No runbook exists. An on-call engineer would not know: what 'normal' sync duration is, how to verify data freshness, what the upstream API SLA is, or how to manually trigger a sync.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["INVENTORY_API_TIMEOUT_MS — request timeout configuration","SYNC_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS — retry limit","SYNC_RETRY_BACKOFF_MS — initial backoff interval"],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Metrics endpoint or StatsD/Prometheus client configuration for emitting sync metrics","Alerting rules: sync_failure_rate > threshold, time_since_last_success > 10m"],"infra_provisioning":["Alerting rules in monitoring system for sync health","Dashboard for sync service operational visibility"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema migrations. However, corrupted stock counts written by a buggy sync cannot be rolled back by redeploying — a data remediation step would be needed."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Service has zero metrics, no health endpoint, and only unstructured console.log output","reason":"Without metrics or a health endpoint, there is no way to: (1) alert when syncs fail, (2) detect stale inventory data, (3) monitor service health via orchestrator health checks, or (4) build an operational dashboard. An on-call engineer cannot assess service health without SSHing into the box and grepping logs. This is not production-grade.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No timeout on outbound API call creates silent cascading failure risk","reason":"A hung upstream connection produces zero log output (no start log, no completion log, no error log). The service appears healthy but is doing nothing. Combined with zero metrics, this failure mode is completely invisible. Must add timeout + metrics before shipping.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":55,"high_confidence_areas":["Rollback safety — no migrations, clean image rollback","Failure mode analysis — the gaps are unambiguous from the service description","Observability gaps — clearly no metrics, no health check, unstructured logging"],"low_confidence_areas":["Database write atomicity — not enough detail on whether writes are transactional","Concurrent sync overlap behavior — unclear if the scheduler prevents overlap","External API contract stability — unknown if the API has versioning or breaking change history"],"assumptions":["The service runs as a containerized workload with a scheduler (cron or setInterval) triggering every 5 minutes","The external inventory API is HTTP-based","The local database is relational and supports transactions","No other service writes to the same stock count data"]}}
